title: The backend shall remain the authoritative source of runtime truth, command arbitration, and applied state publication.
expected_proof_type: published state trace, command log, proof bundle
prohibited_conflations: applied state != observed state | requested state != applied state
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: The UI shall never silently imply authority that it does not possess.
prohibited_conflations: UI state != command authority | authorization != UI enablement | workspace/profile restore != machine state restore
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Every signal shall have stable identity independent of user-facing display name.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Derived signals shall be dependency-tracked and cycle-checked before activation.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Output actions shall be requests subject to arbitration, permissives, interlocks, and safe-state policy rather than direct uncontrolled writes.
prohibited_conflations: applied state != observed state | requested state != applied state
implementation_notes: The first code sprint may model request/arbitration/apply state and rejection reason, but shall not perform physical output writes.

title: Requested, applied, and observed output states shall be distinguishable in runtime behavior and evidence surfaces.
prohibited_conflations: applied state != observed state | requested state != applied state
implementation_notes: Observed mismatch handling is part of the first-slice structural model. Use explicit mismatch state and timing semantics.

title: Historian data and event evidence shall preserve context linking runtime values, commands, alarms, rules, sequences, and diagnostics.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Live and historical review modes shall remain distinct and reversible without losing operator clarity.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Alarm lifecycle shall distinguish event generation, alarm assertion, return-to-normal, latching, shelving, suppression, and acknowledgment.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Acknowledgment shall never erase historical alarm truth and shall remain attributable to actor, session, and time.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Profiles, autosave, and restore shall preserve user/session continuity without silently implying restored live machine state.
prohibited_conflations: UI state != command authority | workspace/profile restore != machine state restore
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Layout persistence and profile restore shall preserve workspace arrangements, subject to current live capability and authorization checks.
prohibited_conflations: UI state != command authority | workspace/profile restore != machine state restore
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Roles and permissions shall be backend-enforced, not merely UI-hidden.
implementation_notes: Only the attribution and backend-vs-UI-boundary scaffolding is expected in the first code sprint; broad policy enforcement remains follow-on.

title: Command authorization decisions shall remain attributable, reviewable, and linked to session, actor, and target action.
implementation_notes: Attributable authorization decisions should have model and evidence hooks before broad enforcement surfaces are built.

title: The shell shall preserve a graph-dominant operating posture with dockable control surfaces, a persistent evidence spine, and recoverable workspace layout.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Graphing shall support live follow, sliding-window live view, whole-history review, explore mode, and explicit return-to-live behavior.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: The graphing surface shall support event, alarm, command, rule, sequence, and other relevant evidence overlays where relationships matter.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Widget language shall consistently distinguish truth quality, authority source, draft/deployed state, requested/observed state, and protection/trip semantics.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Every send-out package shall be auditable by the package-root audit tool and governed package audit specification.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Draft-residue/anti-pattern scanning shall use rule-scoped allowed-pattern logic by document class and shall not permit structural-integrity waivers.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: The package-root master auditor shall perform structure, semantic, contradiction, and duplication audits for hardening packages.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Every release package shall declare package ID, code baseline ID, document baseline ID, compatibility statement, included artifacts, and change summary.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Completion claims shall be supported by explicit proof bundles rather than narrative assertion alone.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Controlled documents, requirement IDs, and cross-references shall remain machine-readable and internally consistent across the active corpus.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Every project-critical term shall have one canonical owner, declared alias policy, and governed anti-conflation treatment.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Material contradictions and semantic ambiguities shall be registered, resolved, or explicitly elevated before downstream implementation relies on them.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Meaningful duplication in normative content shall be classified as intentional, governed boilerplate, shadow definition, or consolidation-required before release-candidate use.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.

title: Future implementation packages shall be anchored to a governed file/module structure with declared boundary rules.
implementation_notes: Stay within the bounded first code sprint defined by the package.
